source: Hengl, T. (2018). Global Landform and Lithology / USGS EcoTapestry

classes.name: Acid Plutonics
classes.description: Coarse-grained intrusive igneous rocks with high silica content, such as granite and granodiorite, which form the cores of many mountain ranges.

classes.name: Acid Volcanics
classes.description: Fine-grained to glassy extrusive igneous rocks rich in silica, including rhyolite and obsidian, often associated with explosive volcanic eruptions.

classes.name: Basic Plutonics
classes.description: Coarse-grained intrusive rocks low in silica and rich in iron and magnesium, such as gabbro and norite, common in oceanic crust and mafic intrusions.

classes.name: Basic Volcanics
classes.description: Dark, fine-grained extrusive rocks such as basalt and andesite that form ocean floors, flood basalt plateaus, and many island chains.

classes.name: Carbonate Sedimentary Rock
classes.description: Limestone, dolomite, and chalk formed from the accumulation of marine organisms or chemical precipitation, often hosting karst landscapes.

classes.name: Evaporite
classes.description: Salt, gypsum, and anhydrite deposits left behind by the evaporation of ancient seas or saline lakes, found in arid basins and subsurface strata.

classes.name: Ice and Glaciers
classes.description: Perennial ice sheets and glaciers covering bedrock, including Greenland, Antarctica, and high-altitude glacier complexes.

classes.name: Intermediate Plutonics
classes.description: Intrusive igneous rocks with silica content between mafic and felsic extremes, such as diorite and tonalite.

classes.name: Intermediate Volcanics
classes.description: Extrusive igneous rocks of intermediate composition including andesite and trachyte, common in subduction zone volcanoes.

classes.name: Metamorphics
classes.description: Rocks recrystallized under heat and pressure, including schist, gneiss, and quartzite, forming the basement of ancient cratons and orogenic belts.

classes.name: Mixed Sedimentary Rock
classes.description: Interbedded or transitional sequences that combine multiple sedimentary rock types and cannot be assigned to a single dominant class.

classes.name: Pyroclastics
classes.description: Fragmental volcanic material such as tuff, breccia, and ignimbrite deposited by explosive eruptions or pyroclastic flows.

classes.name: Siliciclastic Sedimentary
classes.description: Clastic sedimentary rocks composed primarily of silicate mineral grains, including sandstone, siltstone, mudstone, and shale.

classes.name: Unconsolidated Sediment
classes.description: Loose, uncemented deposits of sand, gravel, silt, and clay such as river alluvium, coastal dunes, glacial till, and loess sheets.

classes.name: Undefined
classes.description: Lithology data not available or not classified for this area.
